title: Decompose figures to find area
description: Practice decomposing irregular shapes to find their area.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below shows a large rectangle with a small rectangle cut out of it.


[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the figure?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] $\text{ square units}$
hints:
We can think of the figure as a large rectangle with a small rectangular area removed.


[[☃ image 1]]

First, let's find the area of the large rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}\blueD{\text{Area}} &=\blueD{7} \times \blueD{7}\\
&= \blueD{49} \text { square units}\end{align}$

Next, let's find the area of the small rectangle cut out.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}\purpleC{\text{Area}} &=\purpleC{3} \times \purpleC{3}\\
&= \purpleC{9} \text { square units}\end{align}$

To find the area of the figure, we subtract the area of the smaller rectangle from the area of the larger rectangle.

$\blueD{49}\text{ square units}-\purpleC{9}\text{ square units}=40 \text{ square units}$
The area of the figure is $40\text{ square units}$.

options.alt: An 8-sided figure in the shape of a large rectangle with a small rectangle cut out from the top. The large rectangle has a length of 7 units and a width of 7 units. The space left from the small rectangle has a length of 3 units and a width of 3 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below shows a large rectangle with a small rectangle cut out of it.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the figure?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] $\text{ square units}$
hints:
We can think of the figure as a large rectangle with a small rectangular area removed.

[[☃ image 1]]

First, let's find the area of the large rectangle.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}\blueD{\text{Area}} &=\blueD{9} \times \blueD{8}\\
&= \blueD{72} \text { square units}\end{align}$

Next, let's find the area of the small rectangle cut out.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}\purpleD{\text{Area}} &=\purpleD{4} \times \purpleD{2}\\
&= \purpleD{8} \text { square units}\end{align}$

To find the area of the figure, we subtract the area of the smaller rectangle from the area of the larger rectangle.

$\blueD {72}\text{ square units}-\purpleD{8}\text{ square units}=64 \text{ square units}$
The area of the figure is $64\text{ square units}$.

options.alt: An 8-sided figure in the shape of a large rectangle with a small rectangle cut out from its right side. The large rectangle is 9 units long and 8 units wide. The space left from the small rectangle is 4 units long and 2 units wide.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below shows a large rectangle with a small rectangle cut out of it.


[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the shaded figure?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] $\text{ square cm}$
hints:
We can think of the figure as a large rectangle with a small rectangular area removed.


[[☃ image 1]]

First, let's find the area of the large rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

${\greenD{\text{Area}} = \greenD{9} \times \greenD{8} = \greenD{72} \text{ square cm}}$

Next, let's find the area of the small rectangle cut out.


[[☃ image 1]]

$ {\blueD{\text{Area}} = \blueD{4} \times \blueD{4} = \blueD{16} \text{ square cm}}$

To find the area of the shaded figure, we subtract the area of the smaller rectangle from the area of the larger rectangle.

$ {\greenD{72}\text{ square cm}}$ $-$ ${\blueD{16} \text{ square cm}}$ $=$ $ {56\text{ square cm}}$
The area of the figure is $56$ square centimeters.

options.alt: A large rectangle with a small white rectangle cut out of it. The large rectangle is 9 centimeters long and 8 centimeters wide. The small rectangle is 4 centimeters long and 4 centimeters wide.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below shows a large rectangle with a small rectangle cut out of it.


[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the shaded figure?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] $\text{ square cm}$
hints:
We can think of the figure as a large rectangle with a small rectangular area removed.


[[☃ image 1]]

First, let's find the area of the large rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

${\purpleD{\text{Area}} = \purpleD{8} \times \purpleD{7} = \purpleD{56} \text{ square cm}}$

Next, let's find the area of the small rectangle cut out.


[[☃ image 1]]

$ {\blueD{\text{Area}} = \blueD{3} \times \blueD{2} = \blueD{6} \text{ square cm}}$

To find the area of the shaded figure, we subtract the area of the smaller rectangle from the area of the larger rectangle.

$ {\purpleD{56}\text{ square cm}}-{\blueD{6} \text{ square cm}}= {50\text{ square cm}}$
The area of the figure is $50$ square centimeters.

options.alt: A large rectangle with a smaller rectangle cut out from it. The large rectangle is 8 centimeters long and a 7 centimeters wide. The smaller rectangle is 3 centimeters long and 2 centimeters wide.

assessment_items.question_content:
A large rectangle has side lengths of $8$ meters and $6$ meters.

A smaller rectangle with side lengths of $4$ meters and $2$ meters is cut out of the large rectangle.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the remaining part of the large rectangle?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{ square m}$
hints:
$\underbrace{\purpleD{\text{Area}}}_{\text{shaded figure }}=\underbrace{\blueE{\text{Area}}}_{\text{large rectangle }}-\underbrace{\maroonE{\text{Area}}}_{\text{small rectangle }}$

[[☃ image 1]]

The area of each rectangle equals the base times the height.
The remaining area is $40$ square meters.

options.alt: An 8-sided figure in the shape of a large rectangle with a small rectangle cut out from the top. The large rectangle has a base of 8 meters and a height of 6 meters. The space removed by the small rectangle has a base of 4 meters and a height of 2 meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made of $2$ rectangles.


[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the figure?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{square meters}$
hints:
We can break the figure into two rectangles and find the area of each rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

Let's find the area of the first rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}\redE{\text{Area}} &=\redE{5} \times \redE{3}\\\\
&= \redE{15} \text { square m}\end{align}$  

Next, let's find the area of the second rectangle.    


[[☃ image 2]]

$\begin{align}\blueD{\text{Area}} &=\blueD{2} \times \blueD{9}\\\\
&= \blueD{18} \text { square m}\end{align}$

To find the area of the whole figure, we add the areas of each rectangle.

$\redE{15}\text{ square m}+\blueD{18}\text{ square m}=33\text{ square m}$
The area of the figure is $33$ square meters.

options.alt: A 6-sided shape made up of 2 rectangles. The top rectangle has a length of 3 meters and a width of 5 meters. The bottom rectangle has a length of 9 meters and a width of 2 meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figure below is made of $2$ rectangles.


[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the figure?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]]$\text{ square cm}$
hints:
We can break the figure into two rectangles and find the area of each rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

Let's find the area of the first rectangle.


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueE{\text{Area}} &=\blueE9 \times \blueE{3}\\\\
&= \blueE{27} \text { square cm}\end{align}$  

Next, let's find the area of the second rectangle.    


[[☃ image 2]]

$\begin{align}
\maroonD{\text{Area}} &=\maroonD{2} \times \maroonD{7}\\\\
&= \maroonD{14} \text { square cm}\end{align}$
$\blueE{27}\text{ square cm}+\maroonD{14}\text{ square cm}=41\text{ square cm}$
The area of the figure is $41$ square centimeters.

options.alt: A 6-sided shape made up of two rectangles. The top rectangle has a length of 9 centimeters and a width of 3 centimeters. The bottom rectangle has a length of 2 centimeters and a width of 7 centimeters.

Next, let's find the area of the second rectangle.    


[[☃ image 2]]

 

$\begin{align}\greenD{\text{Area}} &=\greenD{4} \times \greenD{8}\\\\

$\maroonD{27}\text{ square cm}+\greenD{32}\text{ square cm}=59\text{ square cm}$
The area of the figure is $59$ square centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:

**What is the area of the figure?**

hints:
Break the figure into two rectangles.


[[☃ image 1]]

Find the area of each rectangle.    


[[☃ image 1]]

 
${\blueD{\text{Area}} = \blueD{1} \times \blueD{4} = \blueD{4} \text{ square m}}$

[[☃ image 2]]

  
$ {\greenD{\text{Area}} = \greenD{3} \times \greenD{1} = \greenD{3} \text{ square m}}$

To find the area of the whole figure, we add the areas of each rectangle.

$ {\blueD{4}\text{ square m}}$ $+$ ${\greenD{3} \text{ square m}}$ $=$ $ {7\text{ square m}}$
The area of the figure is $7$ square meters.

hints:
Break the figure into two rectangles.


[[☃ image 1]]

Find the area of each rectangle.    


[[☃ image 1]]

 
$\begin{align}\blueD{\text{Area}} &=\blueD{5} \times \blueD{1}\\
&= \blueD{5} \text { square cm}\end{align}$  


[[☃ image 2]]

  
$\begin{align}\purpleC{\text{Area}} &=\purpleC{1} \times \purpleC{3}\\
&= \purpleC{3} \text { square cm}\end{align}$
$\blueD{5}\text{ square cm}+\purpleC{3}\text{ square cm}=8\text{ square cm}$
The area of the figure is $8\text{ square cm}$.

options.alt: An 8-sided shape made up of 2 rectangles. The top rectangle has a length of 5 centimeters and a width of 1 centimeter. The bottom rectangle has a length of 1 centimeter and a width of 3 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Two rectangles are joined together to form a single large area.  The rectangles do not overlap.

The first rectangle has side lengths of $6$ meters and $7$ meters. The second rectangle has side lengths of $3$ meters and $4$ meters.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the combined area?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{ square m}$
hints:
$\underbrace{\purpleD{\text{Area}}}_{\text{shaded figure }}=\underbrace{\blueE{\text{Area}}}_{\text{left rectangle }}+\underbrace{\maroonE{\text{Area}}}_{\text{right rectangle}}$

[[☃ image 1]]

The area of each rectangle equals the base times the height.
The combined area is $54$ square meters.

options.alt: A 6-sided shape made up of 2 rectangles. The rectangle on the left has a base of 6 meters and a height of 7 meters. The rectangle on the right has a base of 3 meters and a height of 4 meters.
